title: Paraffin embedding for mouse tissue
steps: Sacrifice the mouse by an overdose intraperitoneal injection of sodium pentobarbital.
Fix the mouse on the tray, spray with 70% ethanol.
Open the mouse, remove the tissue. For aorta samples, dissect the upper half descending aorta along with heart, rinse in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS).
Transfer the tissue into a 15 ml tube filled with 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in PBS, incubate at 4°C overnight with a gentle agitation. Undetermined
Dehydrate tissue in graded series of ethanol (EtOH): 25% EtOH/PBS, 50% EtOH/PBS, 75% EtOH/dH 2 O, 100% EtOH, 30 minutes each with a gentle agitation at room temperature (RT). 00:30:00
Change the 100% EtOH with a new one, the tissue can be stored for several months at -20°C. Undetermined
Clear tissue twice in xylene, incubate for 1 h with a gentle agitation at RT. 01:00:00
Incubate the tissue in 50/50 xylene/paraffin with an agitation at 60°C for 1 h. 01:00:00 Undetermined
Incubate the tissue in 100% paraffin, three times, 1 h each (or up to overnight) at 60ºC with an agitationn. Undetermined 01:00:00
creator: Muthi Ikawati
